ksas.statement: Knowledge of risk quantification frameworks, including Value at Risk, expected loss models, Monte Carlo simulation, and sensitivity analysis methods.
Knowledge of model evaluation frameworks, including train/validation/test split design, cross-validation, evaluation metric selection, and the conditions under which each metric is appropriate.
Knowledge of NLP evaluation frameworks, including BLEU, ROUGE, BERTScore, and human evaluation methodologies and their appropriate application contexts.
Knowledge of AI testing and red-teaming methods, including adversarial testing, edge case construction, distributional shift testing, and structured failure mode analysis.
Knowledge of AI-specific attack taxonomy, including adversarial examples, model evasion, model extraction, model inversion, membership inference, data poisoning, and prompt injection.
Knowledge of AI evaluation frameworks, including functional performance testing, behavioral testing, red-teaming, fairness evaluation, robustness testing, and safety evaluation.
Knowledge of evaluation dataset design, including test set construction to avoid contamination, representative sampling, edge case coverage, and the measurement of coverage gaps.
Knowledge of AI system documentation standards, including model cards, system cards, datasheets for datasets, and the evaluation evidence standards required for regulatory conformity assessment.

Knowledge of model risk management regulatory requirements, including Federal Reserve SR 11-7, OCC Bulletin 2011-12, and their supervisory expectations for model definition, materiality thresholds, model governance, and independent validation.

Knowledge of model validation methodologies, including conceptual soundness review, outcomes analysis, benchmarking, sensitivity analysis, back-testing, and the distinction between challenger model and champion model approaches.

Knowledge of model inventory management practices, including model tiering by materiality and complexity, risk classification, lifecycle tracking from development through retirement, and the documentation standards required at each stage.
Skill in communicating AI risk findings to technical and non-technical audiences, translating statistical risk measures into business impact terms.
Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.
Skill in designing and executing adversarial testing for AI systems, including red-teaming LLMs, testing model robustness to input perturbation, and documenting vulnerability findings.
Skill in designing comprehensive AI test plans covering functional performance, fairness, robustness, safety, and operational requirements, with defined pass/fail criteria for each dimension.
Skill in communicating AI test results to technical and non-technical stakeholders, including limitation documentation and clear go/no-go recommendations.

Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.

Skill in evaluating model validation findings and remediation plans, assessing whether identified model limitations are acceptable given the model's use case, materiality, and the organization's risk appetite.
Develop and present executive-level data and AI strategy recommendations, including business case, risk assessment, and implementation sequencing rationale.
Conduct threat model assessments for AI systems, identifying AI-specific attack surfaces, threat actors, and applicable mitigating controls.
Design and execute AI system test and evaluation programs, producing documented test plans, results, identified failure modes, and deployment recommendations.
Produce AI system documentation (model cards, evaluation reports) meeting the standards required for governance review, regulatory submission, or external audit.
Operate continuous evaluation processes for deployed AI systems, monitoring for performance degradation, behavioral drift, and emerging failure modes.

Establish and maintain the enterprise model inventory, ensuring all models — including AI/ML models, vendor models, and end-user computing models — are registered, tiered by risk, and subject to appropriate validation cadence.

Conduct or oversee periodic model risk assessments, produce aggregate model risk reporting for senior management and the board, and ensure timely escalation of material model risk findings to appropriate governance bodies.

Define and enforce model development standards, including documentation requirements, testing protocols, approval gates, ongoing monitoring expectations, and the criteria for model retirement or replacement.
Knowledge of enterprise risk management integration, including how data risk, AI risk, model risk, and technology risk roll up into the enterprise risk taxonomy and board-level risk reporting.

Skill in designing risk appetite statements for data and AI activities that are specific enough to guide operational risk decisions yet flexible enough to accommodate business evolution and emerging use cases.
Ability to calibrate risk assessment depth and rigor to the materiality and complexity of the model or AI system under review, allocating assessment resources proportionally to actual risk.
Ability to maintain independence and objectivity when challenging models developed by senior or politically influential teams, providing effective challenge regardless of organizational dynamics.
Ability to synthesize risk findings across multiple models and AI systems into coherent enterprise-level risk narratives suitable for senior management and board reporting.

Ability to adapt risk management frameworks designed for traditional quantitative models to the unique characteristics of AI and ML systems, including foundation models, ensemble methods, and deep learning architectures.
